skill.name: router-stats
skill.description: Display Claude Router usage statistics and cost savings
skill.summary: Display Claude Router usage statistics and cost savings
skill.author: 0xrdan

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown fences, display text, and model names. The confirmed issue is intentional access to a hidden home-directory stats file, which is legitimate for this skill but should be disclosed to users.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Home-Directory File Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read ~/.claude/router-stats.json. This is necessary for the stated purpose, but it accesses a hidden file under the user's home directory and may expose usage patterns or cost estimates.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is explicitly named twice and the skill's steps direct the assistant to read it. The usage is semantically aligned with the skill, so the concern is disclosure and local-file scope rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detection
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable shell command, Ruby code, or user-controlled command construction is present in the reviewed skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are inline code formatting or Markdown code fences. They do not define or invoke a command execution primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography hits do not identify a cryptographic function, algorithm use, key handling, or encoded payload. The flagged lines are metadata and plain output text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain description and display text only. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Detection
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance hit is located inside a formatted cost-savings example. No system information command, host enumeration, process listing, or environment inspection is requested.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is sample display output for delegation savings. It has no semantic connection to system reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Read Tool Access to Hidden Local File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to use the Read tool on a hidden file in the user's home directory. This pattern can reveal local telemetry if the user did not expect the file to be read.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Read tool instruction and hidden path are explicit. The risk is moderated because the path is specific to the advertised router statistics feature.

content.value_statement: Claude Router users need a quick way to understand routing volume and estimated savings. This skill reads the local router stats file and presents route distribution, exceptions, and savings in a clear report.
actual_capabilities: Reads the local Claude Router stats file from ~/.claude/router-stats.json.
Displays total routed queries and route distribution percentages.
Shows tool-intensive, orchestrated, and exception query counts when present.
Calculates route percentages from the stored route totals.
Formats estimated savings, delegation savings, and daily session details.
limitations: Requires the router stats file to exist on the local machine.
Does not create, repair, or update router statistics.
Cost estimates depend on the assumptions documented in the skill.
Only supports the v1.2 stats structure shown in the instructions.

use_cases.title: Audit Routing Mix
use_cases.description: Review how often queries are routed to fast, standard, deep, or orchestrated modes.

use_cases.title: Track Estimated Savings
use_cases.description: Summarize estimated cost savings compared with always using the most capable model.

use_cases.title: Inspect Daily Usage
use_cases.description: Check the latest session totals and savings without manually reading the stats file.

prompt_templates.title: Show My Router Stats
prompt_templates.prompt: Use router-stats to show my current Claude Router usage summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for the default formatted report.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Route Distribution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use router-stats and focus on the route distribution percentages and total routed queries.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants routing mix without extra interpretation.

prompt_templates.title: Review Cost Savings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use router-stats to explain my estimated savings, delegation savings, and total savings.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants the cost section highlighted.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Exceptions and Sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use router-stats to review exceptions, tool-aware routing, and the latest daily session details.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for operational detail across sections.

output_examples.output: A formatted report with all-time routed queries, route distribution, tool-aware routing counts, savings totals, and today's session.

output_examples.input: How much has the router saved?
output_examples.output: A savings-focused summary that separates estimated savings, delegation savings, and total savings using the local stats file.

output_examples.input: Do I have many router exceptions?
output_examples.output: A concise exception summary that lists router meta-query counts and explains that those queries are handled by Opus.

best_practices: Run the skill only when you want the assistant to read the local router stats file.
Review the file path before use if your stats file may contain sensitive usage data.
Compare trends over several sessions before making cost or routing decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a source of billing truth.
Do not expect the skill to modify router configuration or routing rules.
Do not share generated reports publicly if usage patterns are sensitive.

faq.question: What file does this skill read?
faq.answer: It reads ~/.claude/router-stats.json on the local machine.

faq.question: Does it send my stats over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: Can it fix missing or invalid stats?
faq.answer: No. The skill only reports that no stats are available when the file is missing.

faq.question: What savings does it calculate?
faq.answer: It presents estimated savings compared with always using Opus and includes delegation savings when present.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally reads a hidden file in the user's home directory, so users should understand that local-file access.
